source_pr.title: *: make TestPointGetReadLock stable
source_pr.body:
Signed-off-by: Shuaipeng Yu <jackysp@gmail.com>

<!-- Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?

Issue Number: close #xxx <!-- REMOVE this line if no issue to close -->

Problem Summary:
`TestPointGetReadLock` is still not stable

### What is changed and how it works?

Proposal: [xxx](url) <!-- REMOVE this line if not applicable -->

What's Changed:
Move it to serial test and force-reload schema for all lock and unlock DDLs.

How it Works:

### Check List <!--REMOVE the items that are not applicable-->

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- Unit test

### Release note <!-- bugfixes or new feature need a release note -->

- No release note

symptoms: TestPointGetReadLock remains flaky due to stale schema/lock-cache visibility after lock/unlock DDLs and shared global config (`EnableTableLock`) interference.
failure_signature: Intermittent EXPLAIN ANALYZE mismatch in TestPointGetReadLock (e.g., missing expected `num_rpc`) due to cache not released in time / shared state.
root_cause_explanation: TestPointGetReadLock is sensitive to metadata caching and shared global state. Table lock/unlock operations rely on schema/lock-cache updates that are not immediately visible unless the domain/InfoSchema is reloaded, and the test also toggles global config (`EnableTableLock`) which can interfere with other tests if run concurrently. This PR stabilizes the test by moving it to a serial suite, introducing a helper that runs lock/unlock DDLs and forces `domain.Reload()` after each, and restoring global config via `config.RestoreFunc()`.
fix_pattern: Run the test in a serial suite; after each lock/unlock DDL execute `domain.Reload()` to refresh schema/lock cache; wrap global config updates with `config.RestoreFunc()` to avoid cross-test pollution.

evidence: evidence: PR title: *: make TestPointGetReadLock stable
evidence: run test in serial suite + reload schema after each DDL (`mustExecDDL`) to avoid cache-related flakes (executor/point_get_test.go)
